title: Tempting Juicy Korean Almond Bao
description: A briny and juicy Korean dish featuring ground almonds and lamination-puff, perfect for meal prep, made in a kadhai and plant-based friendly.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground almonds, all purpose flour, salt, sugar, and active dry yeast.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased kadhai, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the kadhai over medium heat and add the sesame oil.
Add the chopped scallions and cook until they are softened.
Add the crumbled tofu and cook until it is lightly browned.
Add the soy sauce and gochujang, and stir to combine.
Punch down the risen dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of the tofu and scallion mixture in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
